{
  "edge_cases": [
    {
      "name": "test_edge_depositMaxAmountOverflow",
      "category": "numeric_extreme",
      "test_code": "function test_RevertWhen_depositMaxAmountOverflow() public {\n    // Set non-zero totalAssets and totalShares\n    vault.deposit(100);\n    // Attempt deposit with max uint256 to cause overflow in share calculation\n    vm.expectRevert(stdError.arithmeticError);\n    vault.deposit(type(uint256).max);\n}",
      "expected_behavior": "revert with arithmetic overflow panic",
      "real_world_exploit": "Overflow in share calculation could halt deposits or produce incorrect shares"
    },
    {
      "name": "test_edge_withdrawMaxSharesOverflow",
      "category": "numeric_extreme",
      "test_code": "function test_RevertWhen_withdrawMaxSharesOverflow() public {\n    // Deposit some assets to set non-zero state\n    vault.deposit(100);\n    // Try withdrawing max shares to overflow asset calculation\n    vm.expectRevert(stdError.arithmeticError);\n    vault.withdraw(type(uint256).max);\n}",
      "expected_behavior": "revert with arithmetic overflow panic",
      "real_world_exploit": "Overflow in asset calculation could prevent withdrawals or miscalculate payouts"
    },
    {
      "name": "test_edge_whitelistZeroAddress",
      "category": "address_extreme",
      "test_code": "function test_whitelistZeroAddress() public {\n    // Whitelist zero address; no revert expected\n    vault.whitelistDepositor(address(0), true);\n    assertTrue(vault.allowedDepositors(address(0)));\n}",
      "expected_behavior": "zero address whitelisted successfully",
      "real_world_exploit": "Allows deposits from zero address if msg.sender can be zero, but msg.sender is never zero in practice"
    },
    {
      "name": "test_edge_emergencyWithdrawToZero",
      "category": "address_extreme",
      "test_code": "function test_emergencyWithdrawToZero() public {\n    // Deposit assets first\n    vault.deposit(1000);\n    // Emergency withdraw to zero address; tokens lost\n    vault.emergencyWithdraw(address(0), 1000);\n    assertEq(token.balanceOf(address(0)), 1000);\n}",
      "expected_behavior": "tokens transferred to zero address, lost forever",
      "real_world_exploit": "Owner can accidentally or maliciously burn tokens, irrecoverable loss"
    },
    {
      "name": "test_edge_setFeeOverMax",
      "category": "numeric_extreme",
      "test_code": "function test_RevertWhen_setFeeOverMax() public {\n    // Set fee above MAX_FEE_BPS (1000)\n    vm.expectRevert(Vault.FeeTooHigh.selector);\n    vault.setWithdrawFee(1001);\n}",
      "expected_behavior": "revert with FeeTooHigh",
      "real_world_exploit": "Prevents setting excessive withdrawal fees beyond cap"
    },
    {

      "category": "time_manipulation",
